title: curl API CPU stats look odd?
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
Get CPU stats:
```
curl --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock  http:/d/v3.4.4/libpod/containers/stats?stream=false | jq
...
      "AvgCPU": 2.384087614310953e-07,
      "ContainerID": "cf42945fad9ff8985d92383ff6590c1c591ec1c21334518470cb29c8820f64fc",
      "Name": "my-task",
      "PerCPU": null,
      "CPU": 2.384087614310953e-07,
      "CPUNano": 3968719000,
      "CPUSystemNano": 1904411,
      "DataPoints": 1,
      "SystemNano": 1664669945926897200,
      "MemUsage": 6582272,
      "MemLimit": 16485797888,
      "MemPerc": 0.03992692403921336,
      "NetInput": 0,
      "NetOutput": 0,
      "BlockInput": 0,
      "BlockOutput": 0,
      "PIDs": 12,
      "UpTime": 3968719000,
      "Duration": 3968719000
    }
  ]
}
```
The [documentation](https://docs.podman.io/en/v3.4.4/_static/api.html#tag/containers/operation/ContainersStatsAllLibpod) isn't specific, but by including [this reference](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-stats.1.html) I read that AvgCPU, or CPU are: "Percent CPU, full precision float". The figure(s) reported for CPU utilization are vanishingly small.

When I run the equivalent command using the `podman` command line interface:

```
podman stats --format=json --no-stream
...
  "id": "cf42945fad97",
  "name": "my-task",
  "cpu_time": "4.078823s",
  "cpu_percent": "2.45%",
  "avg_cpu": "2.45%",
  "mem_usage": "6.504MB / 16.49GB",
  "mem_percent": "0.04%",
  "net_io": "-- / --",
  "block_io": "-- / --",
  "pids": "12"
 }
]
```

I see a reasonable value for CPU usage.

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expect the CPU usage figures reported by using Curl and podman command line to be the same.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.3
Built:        Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

```
Listing... Done
podman/jammy,now 3.4.4+ds1-1ubuntu1 amd64 [installed]

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
I have a work-around that I'm very happy with. I make two calls to the API, one second apart, calculate the elapsed nano-seconds, divide through by a billion, and then multiply by 100 to get a percent. i.e.:

```
container.AvgCPU = float64(cs_end.Stats[idx].CPUNano-cs_start.Stats[idx].CPUNano) / 1000000000
container.AvgCPU = containerAvgCPU * 100
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
